pr_summary: This PR adds new functionality to the codebase.
verdict_reason: The review found critical-severity issues requiring attention.

findings.line_content: query = f"SELECT * FROM transactions WHERE user_id = {user_id}"
findings.title: SQL Injection via f-string interpolation
findings.description: This line is vulnerable to SQL injection because it directly interpolates user-supplied values into a SQL query string. An attacker could manipulate the user_id to inject malicious SQL code, potentially leading to data breaches or system compromise.
findings.suggestion: Use parameterized queries instead, e.g., query = "SELECT * FROM transactions WHERE user_id = %s"; db.execute(query, (user_id,))

findings.line_content: db.execute("UPDATE transactions SET status='refunded' WHERE id=" + str(transaction['id']))
findings.title: SQL Injection via string concatenation
findings.description: This line is vulnerable to SQL injection because it concatenates user-supplied values into a SQL query string. An attacker could manipulate the transaction['id'] to inject malicious SQL code, potentially leading to data breaches or system compromise.
findings.suggestion: Use parameterized queries instead, e.g., query = "UPDATE transactions SET status='refunded' WHERE id = %s"; db.execute(query, (transaction['id'],))

findings.title: Hardcoded Secret Key
findings.description: This line exposes a live Stripe secret key, which is a critical security risk. An attacker could use this key to access sensitive payment information or perform unauthorized transactions.
findings.suggestion: Store the secret key securely using environment variables or a secrets management system, and never commit it to version control

findings.title: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) via unescaped template replacement
findings.description: This line is vulnerable to XSS because it inserts user-controlled values into an HTML template without proper escaping. An attacker could manipulate the message to inject malicious JavaScript code, potentially leading to session hijacking or other security issues.
findings.suggestion: Use a templating engine that automatically escapes user-controlled values, or manually escape the message using a library like html.escape()

findings.title: Sensitive value hardcoded
findings.description: The Stripe secret key is hardcoded, which is a security risk. Sensitive values should be stored securely, such as in environment variables or a secure configuration file.
findings.suggestion: Store the Stripe secret key in an environment variable or a secure configuration file, and then load it into the code.

findings.title: Function does multiple things
findings.description: The get_transaction function not only retrieves a transaction but also constructs the SQL query. Consider breaking it down into smaller functions.
findings.suggestion: Consider breaking down the get_transaction function into smaller functions, each with a single responsibility.

findings.title: Unnecessary Synchronous I/O
findings.description: The send_notification function reads a file synchronously, which can block the execution of the program and cause performance issues. This is especially problematic in an asynchronous context.
findings.suggestion: Consider using an asynchronous file reading mechanism or caching the file contents to reduce the performance impact.

positive_observations: The code follows a consistent structure.
Function naming is clear and descriptive.
